REGIONS.name: A Stormy Night & the Sanctuary
REGIONS.tagline: A telepathic plea, a dying uncle, and a princess in the castle dungeon.

steps.t: A thunderstorm wakes you in the dead of night. Princess Zelda's voice reaches your mind: she is locked in the castle dungeon and begs for help. Your uncle takes up his sword, tells you to stay in bed, and slips out into the rain.
This is the prologue — three hearts and no weapon yet. Follow your uncle; everything you need is just ahead.
Get up and leave the house. Head north toward Hyrule Castle through the rainy field, slipping past the patrolling guards.
steps.stuck: From your house, go up and around the castle moat toward the north side. You're looking for the way your uncle took, not the locked front gate.

steps.t: At the castle's edge, find the hidden entrance your uncle used — a hole in the ground behind the bushes that drops into an underground passage.
steps.stuck: On the north-east side of the castle grounds, look for a lone bush by the wall. Pull it (or step into the gap) to reveal a stairway down into the passage.

steps.t: In the passage you find your uncle, badly wounded. With his last strength he gives you his sword and shield and tells you to save the princess.

items.name: Fighter's Sword
items.note: Your uncle's blade — your starting weapon. Swing with the sword button; hold it to charge a Spin Attack.

items.name: Fighter's Shield
items.note: Blocks arrows and shoves back some enemies when you walk into them facing forward.

steps.t: Tap an enemy before it taps you — with only three hearts, two careless hits can end you. Slash bushes and pots for hearts and Rupees as you go.
Climb up into the castle. Cut through the soldiers floor by floor, opening chests for Rupees, Bombs, Arrows, and a small key.
Open the chest holding the Lamp — it lights dark rooms and burns bushes and torches. You'll need it constantly.

items.name: Lamp
items.note: Lights pitch-black rooms and ignites torches and bushes; uses a little magic per flick.

steps.t: Reach the prison block, defeat the guards, and unlock Princess Zelda's cell with the key you found. She joins and follows you.
steps.stuck: The cell key comes from a chest / a guard on the dungeon level just before the cells. Stand on the cell door and it opens once you hold the key.

sections.name: The Hidden Sanctuary
sections.sub: Safe — and the real quest begins

steps.t: Zelda knows a secret way out. In the throne room, pull the hidden lever behind the back wall to open a stairway down into the castle's depths.
steps.stuck: Walk up to the back-right of the throne room and pull the wall ornament that looks like a torch/lever; the wall slides open onto a dark staircase.

steps.t: The basement passage is dark (keep the Lamp handy) and crawling with rats and spike traps. Keep Zelda close — if she lags behind, pause and let her catch up.
Climb out into the Sanctuary. The loyal priest takes Zelda into hiding and explains the danger: the wizard Agahnim has bewitched the king and means to break the ancient seal that holds Ganon.

Your path is set. To stop Agahnim you need the legendary Master Sword — and to claim it you must gather the three Pendants of Virtue from the Eastern Palace, the Desert Palace, and the mountain Tower of Hera.

RUNES.name: Master Sword
RUNES.from: Drawn from its pedestal in the Lost Woods once you hold all three Pendants of Virtue.
RUNES.what: The blade of evil's bane — stronger than your uncle's sword, and at full health it fires a sword beam across the screen.
RUNES.tip: Two later upgrades, the Tempered Sword and the Golden Sword, make it hit even harder. Hold the button to charge a Spin Attack.

RUNES.name: Pegasus Boots
RUNES.from: A gift from the dying soldier / found early once you have the means — hold the dash button to charge.
RUNES.what: Hold A to sprint, then crash through certain weak walls and bushes and stab with a charging thrust.
RUNES.tip: Boots + a few other items combine: dash into cracked walls, knock down stakes, or cross long runs you couldn't walk.

RUNES.name: Moon Pearl
RUNES.from: The reward inside the Tower of Hera, the third Pendant dungeon.
RUNES.what: Keeps you in Link's true form in the Dark World. Without it, crossing over turns you into a helpless pink bunny.
RUNES.tip: Get this before exploring the Dark World freely — it's what makes the second map survivable.

RUNES.name: Hookshot
RUNES.from: Found in the Palace of Darkness, the first Dark World dungeon.
RUNES.what: Fires a chain that grabs posts and pulls you across gaps and water — and yanks or stuns many enemies.
RUNES.tip: If a ledge or chest sits across a pit, the Hookshot is almost always the answer.

RUNES.name: Magic Mirror
RUNES.from: Given by the lost maiden on Death Mountain.
RUNES.what: Warps you from the Dark World back to the matching spot in the Light World — the core trick for crossing between worlds.
RUNES.tip: Reach a place in one world, mirror to the other, move, and cross back to reach spots that look impossible.

RUNES.name: Lamp
RUNES.from: A chest inside Hyrule Castle during the opening rescue.
RUNES.what: Lights dark rooms and burns bushes and torches; many dungeons are pitch-black until you light their torches.
RUNES.tip: If a room is dark and you're taking hits from nothing, light the wall torches with the Lamp.

CHAMPIONS.name: Pendant of Courage
CHAMPIONS.note: Won by clearing the Eastern Palace — one of the three Pendants of Virtue you need to claim the Master Sword.

CHAMPIONS.name: Pendant of Power
CHAMPIONS.note: Won by clearing the Desert Palace in the southwest — the second Pendant of Virtue.

CHAMPIONS.name: Pendant of Wisdom
CHAMPIONS.note: Won atop the Tower of Hera on Death Mountain — the third Pendant, plus the Moon Pearl.

CHAMPIONS.name: Crystal — Palace of Darkness
CHAMPIONS.note: The first rescued Maiden, sealed in a crystal. Seven crystals open the bridge to Ganon's Tower.

CHAMPIONS.name: Crystal — Swamp Palace
CHAMPIONS.note: The second Maiden freed from her crystal prison in the southern swamp.

CHAMPIONS.name: Crystal — Skull Woods
CHAMPIONS.note: The third Maiden, freed from the dungeon hidden in the dark forest of skulls.

CHAMPIONS.name: Crystal — Thieves' Town
CHAMPIONS.note: The fourth Maiden, freed from the village of thieves.

CHAMPIONS.name: Crystal — Ice Palace
CHAMPIONS.note: The fifth Maiden, freed from the frozen island dungeon in the south.

CHAMPIONS.name: Crystal — Misery Mire
CHAMPIONS.note: The sixth Maiden, freed from the swamp dungeon opened by the Ether (or Quake) Medallion.

CHAMPIONS.name: Crystal — Turtle Rock
CHAMPIONS.note: The seventh and final Maiden, freed from the dungeon atop Dark Death Mountain.

ROADMAP.name: The Three Pendants
ROADMAP.note: Courage, Power, and Wisdom — won from the Eastern Palace, the Desert Palace, and the Tower of Hera. Carry all three to the Lost Woods pedestal to draw the Master Sword.

ROADMAP.name: The Seven Crystals
ROADMAP.note: Each Dark World dungeon ends with a Maiden sealed in a crystal. Free all seven to open the bridge to Ganon's Tower and the final fight.

ROADMAP.name: Pieces of Heart
ROADMAP.note: Twenty-four are hidden across both worlds in caves, minigames, and corners. Every four extend your life by a full heart, up to twenty.

ROADMAP.name: The Four Magic Bottles
ROADMAP.note: Bottles hold fairies, potions, or bees. A bottled fairy automatically revives you when you fall — finding all four is worth the detours.

TIPS.name: Two worlds, one map
items: Hyrule has a bright Light World and a twisted Dark World laid over the exact same map. The Magic Mirror warps you from Dark back to Light; special tiles and the Mirror are how you cross between them.
A spot you can't reach in one world can often be reached by crossing to the other world, walking to the matching place, and crossing back.
The Moon Pearl (from the Tower of Hera) keeps you in Link's form in the Dark World. Without it you become a helpless pink bunny over there — get it before exploring freely.

TIPS.name: Sword, then Master Sword
items: Your uncle gives you your first sword in the castle. The Master Sword waits in the Lost Woods once you hold all three Pendants — it's stronger and fires a beam when your hearts are full.
Later upgrades (the Tempered Sword from the dwarf smiths, then the Golden Sword) raise its power again.
Hold the sword button to charge a Spin Attack that hits all around you — perfect for crowds and tougher rooms.

TIPS.name: Tools open the map
items: Most new areas are gated by an item: the Pegasus Boots dash, the Hookshot pulls you across gaps, the Hammer pounds stakes, the Flippers let you swim, the Titan's Mitt lifts dark heavy rocks.
If a screen looks impassable, you're usually missing the tool a dungeon ahead will give you — note it and come back.

Magic items (Fire/Ice Rod, the Cane of Somaria, the capes, the medallions) drain your green magic meter; magic jars and the Magic Potion refill it. The Magic Cape turns you invisible and walks through some hazards.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Pieces of Heart
COLLECTIBLES.note: Every 4 make a full Heart Container — up to 20 hearts of life.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Magic Bottles
COLLECTIBLES.note: Found and traded for across Hyrule; a bottled fairy revives you the moment you fall.
